criteria_checked.note: Delta vs d337a410 is exactly the Rev 3 additions: title->Rev 3; semantic-sweep Finding + Learning; test-adversarial.sh added to Relevant Files; change (j)/DMC-T011; the [Rev 3 lockstep] AC; the bare-assert_eq Risk row; the 'only stale external' Assumption; m6-suite verification-note (38/0); :748 fold into change (d); change (i) extended; frozen-mirror line adds tests/fixtures/hooks-v0.6.5/* (a TIGHTENING); Revisions extended. a1/a2/b/c/e/f/g/h byte-consistent (verified region-by-region vs d337a410 — single-hash reconstruction not usable since multiple regions changed). Approval block preserved verbatim (Status APPROVED, same Approver line + 2026-07-10). Nothing weakened; no deny floor loosened.

Independently re-ran the bare-token sweep (grep assert_eq ask + -> ask across tests/ adapters/ bin/ .claude/ docs/). ONLY test-adversarial.sh:211 is a stale EXTERNAL row. All other hits correctly classified: test-compat.sh:49/:107 and test-codex-shims.sh:514/:515 are Block C consent-tier npm-install ask (LEGIT, remain ask, out of scope); dmc-codex-pretooluse.py:49 is the consumer translation branch (OQ1 leave); bash-radius.py:425 is the NEW internal wrapper 'ask' signal that funnels to the terminal deny. Frozen fixtures (hooks-v0.6.5) carry no such row. No missed stale external row.

The 4-line flip (test-adversarial.sh:211 ask->deny) keeps the row a REAL test: it now guards the wrapper-benign path against a SILENT-ALLOW regression (a real fail-open). It is NOT a tautology. The write-vs-benign PRECISION the row previously carried is fully preserved at the CI-covered module layer: module W3 (:766-769) asserts write-bearing wrapper -> DENY with WRAPPER-EXEC reason; module W4 (:770-774) asserts benign wrapper -> DENY with AMBIGUOUS reason; W5/W6 give unarmed + non-wrapper ALLOW positive controls. No compensating positive row is MANDATORY in the adversarial suite. Change (j)'s relabel to 'undecidable radius fails closed (v1.1.7)' is appropriate. Optional nicety: the adversarial row could also assert the AMBIGUOUS reason, mirroring module W4.

RULING (binds the executor's batch): (1) :75 'otherwise => ASK' MISDESCRIBES the net armed verdict — a benign wrapper now nets DENY via the terminal ambiguous funnel; MUST be clarified in-scope. (2) :426 'ASK dominates' MISDESCRIBES the net verdict (outcome is DENY); MUST be clarified in-scope (e.g. 'ambiguous dominates -> terminal DENY, v1.1.7'). (3) :372-378 _wrapper_verdict docstring: the literal RETURN-TUPLE enumeration ('ask', None) is an ACCURATE internal-signal doc (the function genuinely returns that token) and STAYS; but the OUTCOME clause :378 'falls through to ask (never a silent allow)' MUST gain a note that classify_l1 folds that 'ask' into a net DENY when armed. So I side with the EXECUTOR on :75/:426 and the :378 outcome clause (stale, net-verdict misdescription) and with the PLANNER only on the literal return-token enumeration. All are in the in-scope bin/lib/dmc-bash-radius.py => zero scope cost; this is a refinement of change (d)'s comment lockstep, not a scope expansion.

BINDING: the docs-truthful AC's literal grep ('3 ask|human decides', plan line 325) does NOT catch :75 'otherwise => ASK' or :426 'ASK dominates'. Per the plan's own Rev 3 Learning (sweep SEMANTICALLY, not by literal token), that AC must be verified semantically; clarifying :75, :426, and the :378 outcome clause is a PRECONDITION for honestly marking 'docs truthful in lockstep' met. Leaving them stale would be the anti-patchwork misleading-docs class on a Ring-0 enforcement surface — the same trap that just bit test-adversarial.sh:211.

Status APPROVED preserved verbatim on the same human-gate basis. The test-adversarial lockstep (change j) is the MANDATORY test of the already-human-gated behavioral flip, not a new behavior — so the authorization basis is unchanged. C11-clean: human envelope gate is the grant; the critic verdicts are advisory evidence; the read-only planner did not self-approve.
